# terrible_cisco_mso_ndo_set_rule_policy (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) The description of the Set Rule Policy.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `name` (String) The name of the Set Rule Policy.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `set_additional_community` (String) The append list of communities to configure in the Set Rule Policy. Providing an empty list will remove the `set_additional_community=[]` from the Set Rule Policy.
- `set_as_path` (String) The Autonomous System (AS) path rules to add to the Set Rule Policy. Providing an empty dictionary will remove the `set_as_path={}` rules from the Set Rule Policy.
- `set_community` (String) The community rule to add to the Set Rule Policy. Providing an empty dictionary will remove the `set_community={}` rule from the Set Rule Policy.
- `set_dampening` (String) The dampening rule to add to the Set Rule Policy. Providing an empty dictionary will remove the `set_dampening={}` rule from the Set Rule Policy.
- `set_metric` (String) The metric rule to add to the Set Rule Policy. The value must be between 0 and 999999999. Providing an empty string will remove the `set_metric=""` rule from the Set Rule Policy.
- `set_metric_type` (String) The type of metric rule to add to the Set Rule Policy. Providing an empty string will remove the `set_metric_type=""` from the Set Rule Policy.
- `set_multi_path` (Boolean) The multipath option to add to the Set Rule Policy. Providing a value of `false` will remove multipath from the Set Rule Policy. Enabling multipath requires `set_next_hop_propagate=true` to be enabled.
- `set_next_hop` (String) The next hop rule to add to the Set Rule Policy. The value must be a valid IPv4 or IPv6 address. Providing an empty string will remove the `set_next_hop=""` rule from the Set Rule Policy.
- `set_next_hop_propagate` (Boolean) The next hop propagation value to add to the Set Rule Policy. Providing a value of `false` will remove next hop propagation from the Set Rule Policy. `set_next_hop_propagate` cannot be enabled with `set_route_tag` configured.
- `set_preference` (String) The preference rule to add to the Set Rule Policy. The value must be between 0 and 999999999. Providing an empty string will remove the `set_preference=""` rule from the Set Rule Policy.
- `set_route_tag` (String) The route tag rule to add to the Set Rule Policy. The value must be a number between 0 and 999999999. Providing an empty string will remove the `set_route_tag=""` rule from the Set Rule Policy.
- `set_weight` (String) The weight rule to add to the Set Rule Policy. The value must be a number between 0 and 9999. Providing an empty string will remove the `set_weight=""` rule from the Set Rule Policy.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Determines the desired state of the resource. Use `absent` to remove the resource. Use `query` to list the resource. Use `present` to create or update the resource.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `template` (String) The name of the template. The template must be a tenant template. This parameter or `template_id` is required.
- `template_id` (String) The ID of the tenant template. This parameter or `template` is required.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `uuid` (String) The UUID of the Set Rule Policy. This parameter is required when the `name` attribute needs to be updated.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
